steps.title: Use the ethical contrast
steps.content: The first view says moral goodness depends only on whether the actions themselves are good, regardless of context. Purcell's account of Aztec philosophy is different: actions are judged by how they fit a person's social role and contribute to the community.

steps.title: Choose the context-dependent claim
steps.content: Choice B adds a claim about outsiders judging morality. Choice C assumes context-independent standards the text rejects. Choice D ignores the role-based factor by focusing only on community outcomes. The supported inference is that Aztec thought would reject judging actions apart from social circumstances. The answer is A.
